method_atom: Coherent, large-amplitude population oscillations between two resonantly coupled states of a two-level system, whose frequency and amplitude are tuned by a control parameter sweeping the coupling through resonance, with the coupling strength depending on local density; combined with the principle that a bound composite excitation whose energy lies within a forbidden gap of a periodic band structure is protected from decaying into its constituents.
object_atom: Ultracold 87Rb atoms confined one-per-site in the Mott-insulator phase of an optical lattice, coherently converted to a diatomic molecular state via a magnetic Feshbach resonance near 414 G.
brief_reason: The two-level resonant Rabi-oscillation machinery and the gap-protection-against-dissociation principle are transferable; the specific atom-molecule conversion of Feshbach-resonant 87Rb in an optical-lattice Mott insulator is the domain-specific object.
